text: Monthly training volume is the most important factor in predicting marathon time among recreational runners, more so than training frequency or distance per workout.
The influence of monthly training volume on marathon time is only significant when the running distance per workout exceeds a certain threshold (longest run ≥21 km or average run distance ≥10 km).
For runners whose longest run is under 20 km or average run distance is under 10 km, increasing monthly training volume does not significantly predict faster marathon times.
Training frequency alone does not significantly predict marathon time when monthly training volume is held constant.
For runners training only twice per week or less, average running distance per workout does not significantly correlate with marathon time.
Within training frequency subgroups of three or more sessions per week, monthly training volume, longest run distance, and average run distance all significantly correlate with marathon time.
